mission.title: Install and Configure Kuasar on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kuasar is an efficient container runtime that provides cloud-native, all-scenario container solutions by supporting multiple sandbox techniques including MicroVM, App Kernel, and Wasm. Written in Rust, it integrates with containerd via the Sandbox API.

steps.title: Install prerequisites
steps.description:
Kuasar requires containerd 2.0+ with Sandbox API support. Ensure your nodes have the necessary dependencies.
```bash
# Verify containerd version (2.0+ required)
containerd --version
```

steps.title: Download Kuasar binaries
steps.description:
Download the Kuasar release binaries for your platform.
```bash
curl -fsSL https://github.com/kuasar-io/kuasar/releases/download/v1.0.1-alpha1/kuasar-v1.0.1-alpha1-linux-amd64.tar.gz -o kuasar.tar.gz
tar -xzf kuasar.tar.gz
```

steps.title: Install the Kuasar sandboxer
steps.description:
Install the appropriate sandboxer binary for your use case. Kuasar supports MicroVM (Cloud Hypervisor, QEMU, StratoVirt), Wasm (WasmEdge, Wasmtime), and App Kernel (Quark) sandboxers.
```bash
# Install the vmm-sandboxer for MicroVM support
sudo cp vmm-sandboxer /usr/local/bin/
sudo chmod +x /usr/local/bin/vmm-sandboxer

# Or install the wasm-sandboxer for Wasm support
sudo cp wasm-sandboxer /usr/local/bin/
sudo chmod +x /usr/local/bin/wasm-sandboxer
```

steps.title: Configure containerd to use Kuasar
steps.description:
Update the containerd configuration to register the Kuasar sandboxer plugin.
```bash
cat <<EOF | sudo tee -a /etc/containerd/config.toml
[proxy_plugins.vmm]
  type = "sandbox"
  address = "/run/vmm-sandboxer.sock"
EOF

sudo systemctl restart containerd
```

steps.title: Start the Kuasar sandboxer
steps.description:
Start the sandboxer process as a systemd service or run it directly.
```bash
sudo vmm-sandboxer --listen /run/vmm-sandboxer.sock --dir /var/lib/kuasar/vmm &
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Use crictl to create a test sandbox and container to verify the setup.
```bash
crictl runp --runtime vmm pod-config.json
crictl pods
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the Kuasar sandboxer running alongside containerd, enabling multi-sandbox container workloads on Kubernetes nodes. Pods can be scheduled to use different sandbox types based on RuntimeClass configuration.

uninstall.title: Stop the sandboxer process
uninstall.description: Stop the Kuasar sandboxer service or kill the process.

uninstall.title: Remove containerd configuration
uninstall.description: Remove the Kuasar proxy plugin entry from the containerd config and restart containerd.

upgrade.title: Download the latest release
upgrade.description: Download the latest Kuasar release binaries from GitHub.

upgrade.title: Replace binaries and restart
upgrade.description: Stop the sandboxer, replace binaries, and restart the service.

troubleshooting.title: Sandboxer fails to start
troubleshooting.description: Ensure the listen socket path is writable and containerd 2.0+ is installed. Check if virtualization is enabled on the node for MicroVM sandboxer.

troubleshooting.title: Pods fail to schedule with Kuasar RuntimeClass
troubleshooting.description:
Verify the RuntimeClass is registered and the sandboxer is running.
```bash
kubectl get runtimeclass
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster with containerd 2.0+ and virtualization-enabled nodes (for MicroVM sandboxer).
